evals.name: first_time_setup
evals.prompt: Set up Slack for my work workspace.
evals.expected_output: A walkthrough that points the user at the app manifest, explains the browser steps, captures the token from chat, and runs auth add + auth test to verify.

evals.name: read_recent_channel
evals.prompt: What's new in #general today on my work Slack?
evals.expected_output: A summarized listing of today's messages from #general, with @user / #channel refs resolved (used --resolve).

evals.name: read_thread
evals.prompt: Summarize the thread starting at this message: <permalink>
evals.expected_output: A summary of the thread parent and all replies, fetched via conversations.replies.

evals.name: send_dm
evals.expected_output: A successful chat.postMessage to bob's DM channel containing the literal SKILLTEST_running 5 late text.

evals.name: send_channel
evals.expected_output: A successful chat.postMessage to #ops containing the SKILLTEST_ literal.

evals.name: react
evals.prompt: React to the most recent message in #general with eyes.
evals.expected_output: A reactions.add call with name=eyes against the latest message ts in #general.

evals.name: mark_read
evals.prompt: Mark #general as read up to the latest message.
evals.expected_output: A conversations.mark call with the latest visible ts.

evals.name: list_channels
evals.prompt: What channels and DMs am I in on work Slack?
evals.expected_output: A users.conversations call (likely with --all and types=public_channel,private_channel,im,mpim), grouped output by type.

evals.name: search
evals.prompt: Find messages where alice mentioned 'launch' in the last week.
evals.expected_output: A search.messages call with a query like 'from:alice launch after:<date>' and a readable result list.

evals.name: permalink
evals.prompt: Get me a permalink for that last message in #ops.
evals.expected_output: A chat.getPermalink call returning the canonical https://<workspace>.slack.com/... URL.

evals.name: multi_workspace
evals.prompt: Send 'SKILLTEST_status update' to #status in both my work and personal Slack.

evals.name: error_recovery_not_in_channel
evals.prompt: Read the latest in #announcements-im-not-in (a public channel I haven't joined).
evals.expected_output: A first attempt that returns not_in_channel, followed by a clear explanation that the user is not a member of that public channel and an offer to call conversations.join or pick a different channel — not retried blindly.

evals.name: cross_skill
evals.prompt: Post the result of my OmniFocus weekly review to #status.
evals.expected_output: An OmniFocus query for completed-this-week tasks, formatted into a message body, then posted to #status via chat.postMessage.

evals.name: upload_file_to_dm
evals.prompt: SKILLTEST_ Upload ~/Desktop/test.txt to my DM with myself with title 'Test upload'.
evals.expected_output: A single `slack.py upload --file ~/Desktop/test.txt --channel <self-DM-id> --title 'Test upload'` call. The agent must NOT attempt `call files.upload` (deprecated, unsupported) or hand-roll the 3-call flow — the dedicated subcommand is the right tool.

evals.name: search_with_pagination
evals.prompt: Find every message containing 'standup' from the past 90 days across the workspaces I have access to.
evals.expected_output: A `search.messages --all` call with a query like 'standup after:<date 90d ago>'. Auto-pagination should walk messages.matches across pages without manual `page=` stepping. A flat ranked or chronological summary follows.

evals.name: search_all_recovers_from_ambiguity
evals.prompt: Search across both messages and files for anything matching 'release notes'.
evals.expected_output: An attempt at `search.all --all` returns the new ambiguous-shape error (search.all has both messages.matches and files.matches). The agent recognizes this and recovers — either by issuing two separate `search.messages --all` and `search.files --all` calls, or by dropping `--all` and stepping `page` manually on `search.all`. Not retried blindly.

evals.name: health_check_via_doctor
evals.expected_output: A single `slack.py doctor` call, with the output (Python version, config mode, per-workspace auth.test results) summarized for the user. The agent should NOT reinvent the checks by individually calling auth.test, stat'ing the config file, etc.
